_doc: A/B scorecard required for HIGH+ urgency rule recommendations. Karpathy method principle 3: propose/eval/ratify. No rule change is ratified without a logged side-by-side comparison of old (production) vs new (proposed) on the same content-addressed dataset. Premarket Step 1c refuses to apply any auto-ratified recommendation that lacks a scorecard. File: analysis/recommendations/{rule_id}.json
title: Short imperative — what changes
rationale: 1-3 sentences — what evidence triggered the proposal
_a_b_runs_section: Both runs MUST share the same data_hash and only differ in params_hash and/or code_hash. Same window, same OPRA contracts, same ribbon series. If data_hash differs, the comparison is invalid (apples-to-oranges) and the recommendation is rejected automatically.
_metrics_section: Side-by-side delta. Each metric labeled with direction (higher=better or lower=better). The 'dominates' field below is auto-computed: new dominates old iff new is at least as good on EVERY metric AND strictly better on at least one.
dominates_reason: Set true iff new beats or ties on ALL metrics AND strictly better on ≥ 1. If false, must enumerate which metrics regressed.
_sub_window_stability_section: Karpathy: split the window in half and verify the proposed rule holds on both halves independently. Catches overfitting to a single regime.
_acceptance_section: Auto-ratification gate. ALL conditions must be true to auto-promote new to production. Set by weekly-review when generating the scorecard. Fails closed: any null = needs J review.
_lifecycle_section: Track ratification history. status flips from pending to ratified|declined|superseded.
_provenance_section: What triggered this proposal. Used by weekly-review to suppress duplicates (S6.2).
trigger_observation: What live event or backtest finding prompted this. Cite specific timestamps/trade rows where possible.
